Feature_Name: Inter-gNB-DU LTM Handover UE Context Setup
Feature_Description: This procedure is used for the case when the UE moves from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation for LTM.
Feature_Objectives: To create a UE context and setup one or more data bearers in the candidate gNB-DU for LTM configuration.
To exchange LTM configuration information between gNB-CU and candidate gNB-DU.
Feature_Implementation_Steps: The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s).

If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s).

If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.

Code_Instructions.description: Defines the structure for the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message, specifying its Information Elements (IEs) and their types.
Defines the structure for the F1AP UE Context Setup Response message, specifying its Information Elements (IEs) and their types.
Helper function used to generate the RRC configuration part (e.g., lower layer RRC configurations) that may be included in the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message.
Control Plane (CP) helper function responsible for populating the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message structure with relevant information elements.
Generic F1AP encoding function used to serialize any F1AP message (including UE Context Setup Request/Response) into a byte stream for transmission.
Utility function to safely copy byte arrays, frequently used when handling various Information Elements (IEs) within F1AP messages.
Robust memory allocation utility function that allocates memory and handles potential failures, used when creating F1AP message structures or their components.
Robust memory allocation and initialization utility function that allocates zero-initialized memory and handles potential failures, used for F1AP message structures.
Utility function on the gNB-CU side to retrieve UE-specific F1AP data, essential for populating the F1AP UE Context Setup Request message.
Utility function on the gNB-DU side to retrieve the UE context, necessary for the DU to process and respond to the F1AP UE Context Setup Request.

Piggy_Backed_IES_Info: Not applicable for the scoped messages in this procedure.
